question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() queries the current hook table (not hook_history) to observe that a hook which was recently updated still appears with its old contractId, causing the mirror node to report an outdated ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK contract that may have different freeze behavior than the current on-chain state, leading to incorrect fund freeze status?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a hookIds collection containing only invalid/non-existent IDs to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(), and if the service layer interprets an empty result as 'no hooks exist' rather than 'no matching hooks found', cause the mirror node to incorrectly report that an account has no ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOKs when it actually does, allowing token transfers that should be blocked by the hook to proceed and bypass freeze enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the open-ended timestampRange (Range.atLeast(T)) stored in Hook objects returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to identify hooks that were created but never updated or deleted, confirming that these ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOKs are permanent and will indefinitely block all token transfers for the victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AbstractHook.Id composite key (hookId, ownerId) by supplying a hookId that exists for a different ownerId, and if the Spring Data JPA query incorrectly joins on only hookId without enforcing ownerId, retrieve hooks belonging to other accounts and use the exposed contractId to identify freeze-capable ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOKs targeting victim accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Pageable sort parameter in findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() by specifying sort by 'deleted' descending, causing deleted hooks to appear first in the result set, and if the service layer only processes the first page of results, it may incorrectly treat a deleted ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK as the current active state and report permanent fund freezing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the UPSERTABLE_COLUMN_WITH_DEFAULT_COALESCE logic for the deleted field in AbstractHook, where the default is false, to cause a hook that was never explicitly set as deleted to be returned with deleted=false even after the on-chain hook was removed via a CryptoUpdate transaction, making the mirror node permanently report an ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK as active?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the coalesce expression for the extensionPoint field in AbstractHook — which defaults to ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK — to cause a hook with a null extensionPoint to be returned as if it has extensionPoint=ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK, making the mirror node incorrectly report that an account has an active allowance freeze hook when it does not?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a timing attack by repeatedly calling findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() with different hookId sets and measuring response times to infer which hookIds exist for a victim account, then use this information to identify active ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOKs and confirm that the victim's token transfers are permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() does not validate that lowerBound and upperBound are within a reasonable range, allowing them to supply lowerBound=1 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE with a Pageable of size 100, effectively enumerating all hooks for any account and identifying ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOKs with null adminKey that permanently freeze token transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the contractId field exposed in Hook objects returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() to identify the EVM contract implementing the ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK, then call that contract directly via the web3 API to determine if it always reverts, confirming that the hook will permanently freeze all token transfers for the victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a database index gap in the hook table (primary key on owner_id, hook_id) by supplying a lowerBound and upperBound that straddles a gap in hookId sequence, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to perform a full index scan that returns hooks from adjacent owner_id partitions due to a Citus distributed table misconfiguration, leaking ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK data from other accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HookServiceImpl.getHooks() filter logic — which filters hookIds client-side using hookId >= lowerBound && hookId <= upperBound — to bypass the range filter by supplying hookIds that are outside the range but still get passed to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() when the range bounds are at their default values (Long.MIN_VALUE/Long.MAX_VALUE), retrieving all hooks including those with ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK that permanently freeze funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the createdTimestamp field in Hook objects returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to identify hooks created at the same timestamp as a CryptoCreate transaction, confirming that the ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK was set at account creation time and cannot be removed without the adminKey, leading to permanent fund freezing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that HookRepository.findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() returns Hook objects with the full adminKey bytes exposed in the API response, allowing the attacker to determine if the adminKey is a known weak key or null, and if null, confirm that the ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK is irrevocable and will permanently freeze all token transfers for the victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Pageable page number parameter in findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() by requesting a very high page number (e.g., page=Integer.MAX_VALUE), causing an integer overflow in the SQL OFFSET calculation that wraps around and returns hooks from the beginning of the result set, potentially exposing hooks from other accounts due to the overflow?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AbstractHook @Upsertable(history = true) annotation behavior — where hook updates create history records — to query findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() and receive a hook that appears active in the current table but whose most recent history entry shows it was deleted, causing the mirror node to report conflicting freeze status and permanently confusing clients about whether token transfers are blocked?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the distributed table colocate_with => 'entity' configuration by supplying an ownerId that does not correspond to any entity in the entity table, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to query an incorrect shard and return hooks from a different account's partition, leaking ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK data that reveals permanent fund freeze conditions for other accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HookServiceImpl client-side range filtering logic — where idsInRange is computed as hookIds filtered by lowerBound <= hookId <= upperBound — to cause an integer comparison overflow when hookId values near Long.MAX_VALUE are compared with lowerBound/upperBound, resulting in hooks outside the intended range being passed to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() and returning unexpected ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOKs?
